en_definition: when two classes happen at the same time by mistake
example1: I have a timetable clash between math and chemistry.
example1_ru: У меня накладка в расписании между математикой и химией.
example2: The office fixed the timetable clash within a day.

en_definition: the fact of being present at class or an event
example1: Attendance is checked at the start of every lecture.
example1_ru: Посещаемость проверяется в начале каждой лекции.
example2: Poor attendance can affect your final grade.

en_definition: to not go to a lesson you should attend
example1: He decided to skip class because he felt sick.
example1_ru: Он решил прогулять занятие, потому что плохо себя чувствовал.
example2: Skipping class too often will hurt your grades.

en_definition: a large room where lectures are given
example1: The lecture hall can hold two hundred students.
example1_ru: Лекционный зал вмещает двести студентов.
example2: We sat near the front of the lecture hall.

en_definition: a person who is in the same class as you
example1: My classmate helped me understand the homework.
example1_ru: Мой одногруппник помог мне разобраться с домашним заданием.
example2: She made friends with several classmates this term.

en_definition: a piece of work given by a teacher to do outside class
example1: The homework assignment is due next Monday.
example1_ru: Домашнее задание нужно сдать в следующий понедельник.
example2: This homework assignment took me two hours to finish.

en_definition: expected to happen or be given at a certain time
example1: The essay is due by Friday afternoon.
example1_ru: Эссе нужно сдать до пятницы, до полудня.
example2: Our project report is due next week.

en_definition: when work is handed in after the deadline
example1: Late submissions lose ten points automatically.
example1_ru: За позднюю сдачу автоматически снимают десять баллов.
example2: He apologized for the late submission of his essay.

en_definition: to give something to each person in a group
example1: The teacher handed out the exam papers quietly.
example1_ru: Учитель тихо раздал экзаменационные листы.
example2: She handed out copies of the reading before class.

en_definition: a printed sheet given to students in class
example1: Please keep the handout for tomorrow's discussion.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, сохраните раздаточный материал для завтрашнего обсуждения.
example2: The handout summarized the whole chapter.

en_definition: a white board that teachers write on with markers
example1: The teacher wrote the formula on the whiteboard.
example1_ru: Учитель написал формулу на белой доске.
example2: Please clean the whiteboard after the lesson.

en_definition: a pen with a thick tip used for writing on a whiteboard
example1: The marker pen ran out of ink halfway through.
example1_ru: Маркер закончился на середине.
example2: Bring a spare marker pen to the presentation.

en_definition: a book used for studying a subject
example1: Bring your textbook to every class this term.
example1_ru: Приноси свой учебник на каждое занятие в этом семестре.
example2: The textbook has exercises at the end of each chapter.

en_definition: a book with exercises for students to complete
example1: Finish exercise three in your workbook tonight.
example1_ru: Заверши третье упражнение в рабочей тетради сегодня вечером.
example2: The workbook comes with an answer key at the back.

en_definition: a book of blank pages for writing notes
example1: I write all my lecture notes in this notebook.
example1_ru: Я записываю все конспекты лекций в этот блокнот.
example2: She lost her chemistry notebook last week.

en_definition: a pen used to mark important text in bright color
example1: Use a highlighter to mark the key terms.
example1_ru: Используй маркер-выделитель, чтобы отметить ключевые термины.
example2: My highlighter ran dry before I finished the chapter.

en_definition: a small object used to remove pencil marks
example1: Can I borrow your eraser for a second?
example1_ru: Можно одолжить твой ластик на секунду?
example2: The eraser left marks all over the page.

en_definition: items like pens, paper, and notebooks used for writing
example1: We bought stationery for the new semester.
example1_ru: Мы купили канцтовары к новому семестру.
example2: The shop near campus sells cheap stationery.

en_definition: a card that shows you are a registered student
example1: You need your student ID to enter the library.
example1_ru: Тебе нужен студенческий билет, чтобы войти в библиотеку.
example2: She forgot her student ID at the dormitory.

en_definition: the first week when new students learn about the university
example1: During orientation week, we toured the whole campus.
example1_ru: Во время ознакомительной недели мы обошли весь кампус.
example2: Orientation week helped me find my classrooms easily.

en_definition: a staff member who helps students choose courses and plan studies
example1: My academic advisor suggested I take an extra course.
example1_ru: Мой академический консультант посоветовал мне взять дополнительный курс.
example2: Book a meeting with your academic advisor early.

en_definition: a unit used to measure how much a course counts toward a degree
example1: This course is worth three credit hours.
example1_ru: Этот курс стоит три кредитных часа.
example2: Students need enough credit hours to graduate.

en_definition: a subject that all students must take
example1: Calculus is a core course for engineering students.
example1_ru: Матанализ — обязательный курс для студентов инженерных специальностей.
example2: We finished all our core courses by second year.

en_definition: a subject that students can choose to study
example1: I picked an elective course about world history.
example1_ru: Я выбрал курс по выбору о мировой истории.
example2: Each student must choose one elective course per semester.

en_definition: a document listing the topics covered in a course
example1: The syllabus lists all readings for the semester.
example1_ru: Программа курса перечисляет все материалы для чтения на семестр.
example2: Check the syllabus before you buy your books.

en_definition: a long written assignment done during a semester
example1: My term paper is about renewable energy.
example1_ru: Моя курсовая работа о возобновляемой энергии.
example2: The term paper must be at least ten pages long.

en_definition: to copy someone else's work and present it as your own
example1: Never plagiarize; always cite your sources properly.
example1_ru: Никогда не занимайся плагиатом; всегда правильно указывай источники.
example2: Students who plagiarize can fail the whole course.

en_definition: to check written text carefully for mistakes before submitting it
example1: Please proofread your essay before you submit it.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, вычитай своё эссе перед сдачей.
example2: She proofread the report twice for spelling errors.

en_definition: the system used to turn scores into letter or number grades
example1: Our grading scale runs from A to F.
example1_ru: Наша система оценивания идёт от A до F.
example2: The teacher explained the grading scale on the first day.

en_definition: the minimum score needed to pass a test or course
example1: The pass mark for this exam is sixty percent.
example1_ru: Проходной балл на этом экзамене — шестьдесят процентов.
example2: He barely reached the pass mark in physics.

en_definition: to take an exam again after failing it
example1: She had to retake the final exam in June.
example1_ru: Ей пришлось пересдавать финальный экзамен в июне.
example2: Students can retake one failed course per year.

en_definition: to study intensely in a short time before an exam
example1: He crammed all night before the biology test.
example1_ru: Он зубрил всю ночь перед тестом по биологии.
example2: Cramming rarely works better than steady studying.

en_definition: to look over your class notes again to remember the material
example1: We need to review notes from chapter five before the quiz.
example1_ru: Нам нужно повторить конспекты по пятой главе перед контрольной.
example2: She reviews her notes every evening before bed.

en_definition: a practice exam that is similar to the real one
example1: We took a mock exam last week to prepare.
example1_ru: На прошлой неделе мы писали пробный экзамен для подготовки.
example2: The mock exam helped me manage my time better.

en_definition: a person who supervises students during an exam
example1: The invigilator collected all the phones before the test.
example1_ru: Наблюдатель собрал все телефоны перед тестом.
example2: An invigilator walked around the room during the exam.

en_definition: a piece of paper where you write your exam answers
example1: Write your name on the answer sheet first.
example1_ru: Сначала напиши своё имя на листе с ответами.
example2: She filled in the answer sheet in pencil.

en_definition: a type of question with several answer options to pick from
example1: Half the test was multiple choice questions.
example1_ru: Половина теста состояла из вопросов с вариантами ответа.
example2: I finished the multiple choice section quickly.

en_definition: an exam question that requires a written, detailed answer
example1: The essay question asked us to compare two theories.
example1_ru: Вопрос с развёрнутым ответом просил сравнить две теории.
example2: She spent thirty minutes on the essay question alone.

en_definition: a formal request for an official record of your grades
example1: I submitted a transcript request to the office.
example1_ru: Я подал запрос выписки оценок в деканат.
example2: The transcript request takes about five working days.

en_definition: a document you fill out to register for a school or course
example1: Fill in the enrollment form before the deadline.
example1_ru: Заполни форму зачисления до истечения срока.
example2: The enrollment form needs your passport number.

en_definition: a document used to formally request something, like admission
example1: She submitted the application form online.
example1_ru: Она подала форму заявления онлайн.
example2: The application form asked for two references.

en_definition: a person's name written by hand in a special way
example1: Please put your signature at the bottom of the page.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, поставьте подпись внизу страницы.
example2: The document needs both signatures to be valid.

en_definition: an official mark put on a document to show it is approved
example1: The document needs an official stamp from the office.
example1_ru: Документу нужна официальная печать из деканата.
example2: She got the visa stamped at the embassy.

en_definition: to have an official confirm that a document is genuine
example1: We had the translation notarized before submitting it.
example1_ru: Мы заверили перевод нотариально перед подачей.
example2: The university asked for a notarized copy of the diploma.

en_definition: an official copy of a document that has been checked as genuine
example1: Bring a certified copy of your birth certificate.
example1_ru: Принесите заверенную копию своего свидетельства о рождении.
example2: The office kept a certified copy of my diploma.

en_definition: the first, authentic version of a paper, not a copy
example1: You must show the original document at check-in.
example1_ru: Вы должны показать оригинал документа при регистрации.
example2: Keep the original document safe in your bag.

en_definition: an exact copy of a document made by a machine
example1: Make a photocopy of your passport before the trip.
example1_ru: Сделай фотокопию паспорта перед поездкой.
example2: The office keeps a photocopy of every application.

en_definition: to use a machine to turn a paper document into a digital file
example1: Please scan the form and email it to me.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, отсканируй форму и отправь мне по почте.
example2: She scanned her passport for the online application.

en_definition: to add a document to an email or message
example1: Don't forget to attach a file with your resume.
example1_ru: Не забудь прикрепить файл со своим резюме.
example2: I attached a file, but it didn't send correctly.

en_definition: how fast data can be received from the internet
example1: The download speed here is very slow at night.
example1_ru: Скорость загрузки здесь очень медленная по ночам.
example2: A faster download speed makes video calls smoother.

en_definition: internet access provided through a phone network
example1: I ran out of mobile data before the end of the month.
example1_ru: У меня закончился мобильный интернет до конца месяца.
example2: Turn off mobile data to save your monthly plan.

en_definition: a small card in a phone that connects it to a network
example1: I bought a new sim card when I arrived.
example1_ru: Я купил новую сим-карту, когда приехал.
example2: Her sim card stopped working after the trip abroad.

en_definition: to add more money or credit to an account
example1: I need to top up my phone before calling home.
example1_ru: Мне нужно пополнить баланс телефона перед звонком домой.
example2: You can top up your card at any station.

en_definition: a computer program used on a phone or tablet
example1: This app helps me track my daily study time.
example1_ru: Это приложение помогает мне отслеживать время учёбы каждый день.
example2: I deleted the app because it used too much memory.

en_definition: a short message that appears on your phone or computer
example1: I turned off notifications during the lecture.
example1_ru: Я отключил уведомления во время лекции.
example2: A notification reminded her about the deadline.

en_definition: a picture of what is shown on your screen
example1: Send me a screenshot of the error message.
example1_ru: Отправь мне скриншот сообщения об ошибке.
example2: He took a screenshot of the schedule for later.

en_definition: a device used to charge the battery of a phone or laptop
example1: I forgot my charger at the dormitory again.
example1_ru: Я снова забыл зарядное устройство в общежитии.
example2: Can I borrow your charger for ten minutes?

en_definition: how long a device can work before it needs charging
example1: My phone's battery life is much shorter than before.
example1_ru: Время работы батареи моего телефона намного короче, чем раньше.
example2: The new laptop has excellent battery life.

en_definition: a portable device used to charge phones without a plug
example1: I always carry a power bank in my backpack.
example1_ru: Я всегда ношу повербанк в рюкзаке.
example2: Her power bank saved us during the long trip.

en_definition: the process of choosing a new password after forgetting the old one
example1: I requested a password reset for my student account.
example1_ru: Я запросил сброс пароля для своего студенческого аккаунта.
example2: The password reset email never arrived in my inbox.

en_definition: the name you use to log in to an account
example1: Enter your username and password to log in.
example1_ru: Введите имя пользователя и пароль для входа.
example2: I forgot my username for the university system.

en_definition: to end a session on a computer or website
example1: Always log out after using a shared computer.
example1_ru: Всегда выходи из аккаунта после использования общего компьютера.
example2: She forgot to log out of the library computer.

en_definition: to install the newest version of a computer program
example1: Remember to update the software before the meeting.
example1_ru: Не забудь обновить программное обеспечение перед встречей.
example2: The laptop asked to update software this morning.

en_definition: when a computer or app suddenly stops working
example1: My laptop crashed while I was writing the essay.
example1_ru: Мой ноутбук завис, пока я писал эссе.
example2: The app crashes every time I open large files.

en_definition: to turn a device off and on again
example1: Try to restart your phone if it freezes.
example1_ru: Попробуй перезагрузить телефон, если он завис.
example2: We restarted the router to fix the connection.

en_definition: a phone or computer call where you can see each other
example1: We made a video call to our family back home.
example1_ru: Мы сделали видеозвонок нашей семье домой.
example2: The video call kept freezing because of slow internet.

en_definition: to turn off the sound so others cannot hear you
example1: Please mute your microphone during the presentation.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, выключи звук микрофона во время презентации.
example2: She muted her phone before entering the exam room.

en_definition: to check something again to make sure it is correct
example1: Double-check your answers before you submit the test.
example1_ru: Перепроверь свои ответы перед сдачей теста.
example2: I always double-check the address before sending a package.

en_definition: a polite way to ask someone to say something again
example1: Sorry, could you repeat that a bit more slowly?
example1_ru: Извините, не могли бы вы повторить это немного медленнее?
example2: She asked could you repeat that, since the room was noisy.

en_definition: to talk more loudly so people can hear you
example1: Could you speak up? I can't hear you well.
example1_ru: Не могли бы вы говорить громче? Я плохо вас слышу.
example2: The teacher asked the shy student to speak up.

en_definition: to contact someone, especially by phone or message
example1: Please get in touch if you have any questions.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, свяжитесь со мной, если возникнут вопросы.
example2: I got in touch with my advisor about the deadline.

en_definition: to continue contacting someone regularly
example1: We promised to keep in touch after graduation.
example1_ru: Мы пообещали поддерживать связь после выпуска.
example2: Let us keep in touch through video calls.

en_definition: a situation where people do not understand each other correctly
example1: There was a misunderstanding about the meeting time.
example1_ru: Произошло недопонимание насчёт времени встречи.
example2: The misunderstanding was cleared up after a short talk.

en_definition: to say sorry for something you did wrong
example1: He apologized for arriving late to the meeting.
example1_ru: Он извинился за опоздание на встречу.
example2: I want to apologize for the confusing email.

en_definition: to start talking while someone else is still speaking
example1: Sorry to interrupt, but I have a quick question.
example1_ru: Извините, что перебиваю, но у меня короткий вопрос.
example2: Please don't interrupt while the teacher is explaining.

en_definition: to move your head up and down to show agreement
example1: She nodded to show she understood the instructions.
example1_ru: Она кивнула, показывая, что поняла инструкции.
example2: He nodded politely and continued listening.

en_definition: to say hello to someone when you meet them
example1: The staff greeted every new student warmly.
example1_ru: Сотрудники тепло приветствовали каждого нового студента.
example2: She greeted her neighbor with a smile.

en_definition: to tell someone your name when meeting them for the first time
example1: Let us go around and introduce yourself to the class.
example1_ru: Давайте по очереди представимся классу.
example2: He was too shy to introduce himself at the party.

en_definition: casual conversation about unimportant topics
example1: We made small talk while waiting for the bus.
example1_ru: Мы вели светскую беседу, пока ждали автобус.
example2: She isn't very good at small talk with strangers.

en_definition: having good manners and being respectful to others
example1: It's polite to say thank you after receiving help.
example1_ru: Вежливо говорить спасибо после получения помощи.
example2: The staff at the office were very polite.

en_definition: not polite; showing bad manners
example1: It's rude to interrupt someone who is speaking.
example1_ru: Грубо перебивать того, кто говорит.
example2: He felt embarrassed after being rude to the waiter.

en_definition: making you feel shy or uncomfortable
example1: Forgetting his name was really embarrassing.
example1_ru: Забыть его имя было по-настоящему неловко.
example2: It was an embarrassing moment during the presentation.

en_definition: feeling sad because you miss your home and family
example1: She felt homesick during her first month abroad.
example1_ru: Она тосковала по дому в первый месяц за границей.
example2: Video calls with family help when I feel homesick.

en_definition: feeling like you have too much to deal with
example1: I felt overwhelmed by all the new information.
example1_ru: Я чувствовал себя перегруженным всей этой новой информацией.
example2: New students often feel overwhelmed in the first week.

en_definition: feeling less worried after a problem is solved
example1: I felt relieved when the exam results arrived.
example1_ru: Я почувствовал облегчение, когда пришли результаты экзамена.
example2: She was relieved to find her lost passport.

en_definition: feeling annoyed because something is not going well
example1: He was frustrated with the slow internet connection.
example1_ru: Он был раздражён из-за медленного интернет-соединения.
example2: She got frustrated trying to fix the printer.

en_definition: wanting to know or learn more about something
example1: The students were curious about Chinese culture.
example1_ru: Студенты были любопытны насчёт китайской культуры.
example2: He is always curious about how machines work.

en_definition: not able to understand or think clearly
example1: I was confused by the new grading system.
example1_ru: Я запутался в новой системе оценивания.
example2: She looked confused after the fast explanation.

en_definition: having a strong reason to work hard toward a goal
example1: He felt motivated after talking to his advisor.
example1_ru: Он почувствовал мотивацию после разговора со своим куратором.
example2: Good grades kept her motivated all semester.

en_definition: feeling worried or nervous about something
example1: She felt anxious before her first exam abroad.
example1_ru: Она чувствовала тревогу перед своим первым экзаменом за границей.
example2: He was anxious about speaking in front of the class.

en_definition: to become less upset, worried, or angry
example1: Take a deep breath and calm down before the interview.
example1_ru: Сделай глубокий вдох и успокойся перед собеседованием.
example2: It took her a few minutes to calm down after the news.

en_definition: feeling relaxed and at ease, not stressed
example1: I feel comfortable speaking English with my classmates now.
example1_ru: Теперь мне комфортно говорить по-английски со своими одногруппниками.
example2: The new dorm room is small but comfortable.

en_definition: not feeling relaxed or physically at ease
example1: The chairs in the library are quite uncomfortable.
example1_ru: Стулья в библиотеке довольно неудобные.
example2: She felt uncomfortable answering personal questions.

en_definition: feeling pleased about an achievement or someone's success
example1: My parents are proud of my exam results.
example1_ru: Мои родители гордятся моими результатами экзамена.
example2: He was proud of finishing the marathon.

en_definition: feeling sad because something was not as good as expected
example1: She was disappointed with her low test score.
example1_ru: Она была разочарована низким результатом теста.
example2: We were disappointed with the weather during the trip.

en_definition: no longer interested in something after a long time
example1: He got bored of the same lunch every day.
example1_ru: Ему наскучил один и тот же обед каждый день.
example2: I'm bored of watching the same show again.

en_definition: easily annoyed and bad-tempered
example1: He gets grumpy when he doesn't sleep enough.
example1_ru: Он становится сварливым, когда недосыпает.
example2: The grumpy driver honked at every red light.

en_definition: in a happy and positive way
example1: She waved cheerfully at her friends across the street.
example1_ru: Она весело помахала своим друзьям через улицу.
example2: He greeted every customer cheerfully all morning.

en_definition: to become familiar and comfortable with something over time
example1: It took a month to get used to the new schedule.
example1_ru: Понадобился месяц, чтобы привыкнуть к новому расписанию.
example2: You'll get used to the local food eventually.

en_definition: to feel sad because you are away from your family and country
example1: I miss home the most during the holidays.
example1_ru: Я больше всего скучаю по дому во время праздников.
example2: He calls his parents every week because he misses home.
